content: Methanol poisoning causes metabolic acidosis, visual disturbances (blurred vision, blindness), CNS depression, and potentially death. Methanol is metabolized to formaldehyde and formic acid, which cause tissue damage. Symptoms appear 12-24 hours after ingestion. Treatment includes fomepizole or ethanol to block metabolism, bicarbonate for acidosis, folate supplementation, and hemodialysis in severe cases. Immediate emergency care is critical.
